repair_guidance: Do not mutate graph and do not construct a set. Use graph.get(node, ()) for neighbor-only nodes. Preserve first-discovery order in an ordered_nodes list, use list-valued adjacency, compute indegree for every ordered node, always select the earliest ready node in ordered_nodes, and compare result length with ordered_nodes rather than graph. Keep the complete function under 50 lines.
prompt: Write a concise Python function named stable_topological_sort that accepts a mapping of nodes to outgoing neighbors. Do not mutate graph and do not use a set. Preserve input and first-discovery order whenever multiple nodes are ready, include neighbor-only nodes, use graph.get for them, select the earliest ready node in the complete ordered node list, and raise ValueError only when that complete graph contains a cycle. Return only one complete Python code block under 50 lines.
